texto: Perhaps the day will come where the validity of one's spirituality will be judged not by the correctness of one's theology but by the authenticity of one's spiritual life. When that day comes, an authentically spiritual Buddhist and an authentically spiritual Christian may find that they have more in common with each other than they do with those in their respective religions who have failed to develop their spirituality. (Beyond Religion, p. 98)
explicacao: Orientação direta de David N. Elkins sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. A afirmação “Perhaps the day will come where the validity of one's spirituality will be judged not by the corr…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a fe, authenticity, buddhism, útil em momentos de buddhism e christianity.

frase_original: Perhaps the day will come where the validity of one's spirituality will be judged not by the correctness of one's theology but by the authenticity of one's spiritual life. When that day comes, an authentically spiritual Buddhist and an authentically spiritual Christian may find that they have more in common with each other than they do with those in their respective religions who have failed to develop their spirituality. texto: The same sensitivity that opens artists to Being also makes them vulnerable to the dark powers of non-Being. It is no accident that many creative people--including Dante, Pascal, Goethe, Nietzsche, Kierkegaard, Beethoven, Rilke, Blake, and Van Gogh--struggled with depression, anxiety, and despair. They paid a heavy price to wrest their gifts from the clutches of non-Being. But this is what true artists do: they make their own frayed lives the cable for the surges of power generated in the creative force fields of Being and non-Being. (Beyond Religion, p. 124)
explicacao: Citação de David N. Elkins sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “The same sensitivity that opens artists to Being also makes them vulnerable to the dark powers of…” articula fe, anxiety, art em registro de fe, com relevância para contextos como reflexao-noturna e art.

texto: While art should never become exclusionary and elitist, any culture which fails to support its artists is only contributing to its own impoverishment. (Beyond Religion, p. 122)
explicacao: Orientação direta de David N. Elkins sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “While art should never become exclusionary and elitist, any culture which fails to support its ar…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a fe, art, culture, útil em momentos de culture.

texto: In my more rebellious days I tried to doubt the existence of the sacred, but the universe kept dancing and life kept writing poetry across my life. (Beyond Religion, p. 81)
explicacao: Citação de David N.
